metadata.title: newspaper clipping of Dr. Upham's trip to Japan, 1930
metadata.creator: anonymous
metadata.description: A newspaper clipping from the Journal-News detailing Dr. A. H. Upham's planned trip to Japan for a sabbatical to study educational methods and visit alumni. The Upham family will visit several countries, including Japan and India.
metadata.transcript:
[HANDWRITTEN] PAGE TWO

OXFORD

Ralph McGinnis, correspondent, phone Oxford 256-J or 560-L. Miss Jeni Van Ausdal, society and personals, phone Oxford 596. News items may be left at Honack's Studio. J. C. Byrne Drug company, business agent for the Journal-News Phone Oxford 41 for subscriptions and advertisements.

DR. UPHAM AND FAMILY PLANNING TRIP TO JAPAN

To Leave San Francisco Next Week For Voyage; Duties On Campus Placed In Hands Of A. K. Morris

Special To The Journal-News. Oxford, Nov. 16. Dr. A. H. Upham, Miami president, Mrs. Upham, and daughter, Peggy, will leave this week for a winter vacation which will be in the nature of a Sabbatical leave. The Uphams plan to leave San Francisco early next week and will visit Japan, Hong Kong, the Philippines, Java, India, Egypt, and the Mediterranean countries, returning via New York. Dr. Upham will study educational methods in foreign universities and will visit Miami alumni living abroad. They will be met in Honolulu by Mrs. W. H. Shideler, and later will meet Dr. Shideler, who is touring the world, in Bombay. The Uphams plan to renew old acquaintances among Miami alumni now living in Japan, the Philippines, and other countries. Dr. Upham returned to Miami in 1927 after an absence of eight years as president of the University of Idaho. A. K. Morris, vice president, will act for him in his absence.
